title: India considering naval escort for ships in Strait of Hormuz amid Iran war: Report
content: India is contemplating deploying naval ships to escort its merchant vessels through the Strait of Hormuz amidst escalating Middle East tensions. This consideration follows urgent pleas from Indian shipowners seeking protection for their commercial fleets navigating the crucial, now high-risk, waterway.

title: Why ICC penalised Arshdeep Singh: Rules, sanctions, punishment - All you need to know
content: India pacer Arshdeep Singh has been fined 15% of his match fee and received one demerit point for aggressively throwing the ball at Daryl Mitchell during the T20 World Cup final. This action breached ICC Code of Conduct Article 2.9, relating to inappropriate or dangerous ball throwing. Arshdeep accepted the sanction, marking his first offense within a 24-month period.

title: The Iran war’s hidden crisis: Why water could matter more than oil
content: Amidst the Iran war's focus on oil, a critical vulnerability, water, has been overlooked. Gulf nations heavily depend on desalination plants, now facing damage from missile and drone strikes. The article highlights the potential for a severe water crisis, with international law prohibiting attacks on such vital infrastructure.

title: As Canada makes u-turn on TikTok ban, minister puts it on approach taken by Europe
content: Canada has reversed its ban on TikTok's local operations, allowing the social media giant to continue business under strict, EU-inspired security rules. This decision follows a national security review, with new undertakings to protect Canadian user data and minors. The move also aims to preserve Canadian jobs and boost the country's cultural sector.

title: Trump is like every warmongering US president - except for one thing
content: But perhaps the key to understanding why Trump behaves the way he does is because of where he sits. Trump, the former real estate developer, views the world as property. Gaza? A premium property just waiting to be rid of war. Greenland rich in resources that can help America. Venezuela? Oil that can be used by America. Shakespeare said the world is a stage. For Trump, it’s prime real estate.

title: 'Want to win more trophies': Suryakumar Yadav's big statement after T20 World Cup glory
content: Suryakumar Yadav, fresh off captaining India to the T20 World Cup title, expressed his elation and reflected on the team's winning streak. He aims to build on this momentum, targeting Olympic gold in 2028 and further ICC trophies, while acknowledging the honor of being compared to legendary captains like Dhoni and Rohit.

title: Army columns deployed in Meghalaya after 2 killed in police firing; curfew clamped
content: Army columns were deployed in Meghalaya's West Garo Hills after two died in security forces' firing during violent clashes over GHADC election nominations. Tensions flared between tribal and non-tribal groups regarding candidate eligibility. A curfew is in effect, and internet services are suspended to quell unrest and restore order.

title: Anthropic takes US govt to court; CFO says: After President Trump’s social media posts, a major ...
content: Anthropic CEO apologized for a memo after talks with the Pentagon broke down, only to file a lawsuit against the Trump administration days later. The company seeks to overturn a national security threat designation, claiming it jeopardizes billions in revenue and irreparably harms its business and investor confidence.

title: Obama thinks they are real, Trump has 'UFO speech ready': Are we close to finding aliens?
content: The possibility of alien life has moved from fiction to scientific inquiry, fueled by government interest in UAP reports. While scientists haven't confirmed alien spacecraft, the universe's vastness sparks questions about life beyond Earth.  Despite discoveries, the Fermi Paradox—the lack of evidence for alien civilizations—remains a profound cosmic puzzle.

title: Amazon sends complaint letter to FCC against Elon Musk's Starlink
content: Amazon's Project Kuiper has urged the FCC to reject SpaceX's proposal for a million-satellite constellation, citing a lack of essential technical and space safety details. Amazon argues the plan is speculative, would take centuries to deploy, and could monopolize orbital resources, hindering future space endeavors.

title: US-Iran war: How can India shock-proof itself against future oil, LPG, LNG crisis
content: The Middle East crisis has brought to fore with clarity an important fact - while India says it is comfortably placed on its oil, LPG, and LNG supplies - there is need to step up pace on long-term plans.

title: Now Rs 131 cr: How much has BCCI rewarded India for ICC triumphs?
content: After India’s T20 World Cup 2026 triumph, the BCCI announced a Rs 131 crore reward for the team and support staff. Each of the 15 players is expected to receive Rs 6 crore, with the rest shared among staff. India beat New Zealand by 96 runs in the final, becoming the first team to retain the title and win the tournament three times.

title: Dallas Cowboys eye trade with Pittsburgh Steelers for veteran linebacker
content: The Dallas Cowboys are reportedly exploring trade options with the Pittsburgh Steelers as they search for help at linebacker. After missing several free agent targets, Dallas is considering a deal for a two-time Pro Bowl defender entering the final year of his contract to strengthen its defense.

title: Tennessee Titans linked to $67M San Francisco 49ers wide receiver trade
content: Jauan Jennings faces an uncertain future with the San Francisco 49ers after Tampa Bay Buccaneers legendary wide receiver Mike Evans was brought in. Jennings is a Tennessee native. He is now a coveted free agent. The Tennessee Titans are considered a strong possibility for him. He could provide veteran leadership for second-year quarterback Cam Ward on the gridiron. Jennings has accumulated significant yards and touchdowns during his time with the Niners.

title: TACO vs Tehran: Who will blink first—Trump or Khamenei?
content: On Day 11 of the US-Iran war, President Trump claims devastating military losses and a swift end, while Iran signals prolonged resistance. This conflict is evolving into a test of endurance, with Iran aiming to outlast Washington and Israel through attrition and disruption, while Trump faces domestic pressure from rising oil prices and potential midterm backlash.

title: Lakshya admits self-expectations added to All England 'heartbreak'
content: India’s Lakshya Sen finished runner-up at the 2026 All England Open, losing the final to Taiwan’s Lin Chun-Yi. Fatigue from a gruelling 97-minute semi-final and a foot blister affected his performance. Despite disappointment, Sen reflected positively on the week, highlighting his improved belief and expectations compared to 2022 after defeating several top-ranked opponents.

title: Only for India? Trump's msg to Europe on easing of Russian oil sanctions
content: The United States has informed its European partners that any additional easing of sanctions on Russian crude would largely be confined to shipments destined for India.

title: 'Willing to talk to Iran', says Trump, but warns new leader won't 'live in peace'
content: Trump said that the Iran war was going to "end soon", without providing any specific timeline. He also warned Tehran that if they tried "anything cute", the US would end the nation. He called the Iran war a "short term excursion." During a CBS interview, he said that the war was "very complete" and warned Iran against doing "anything cute".

title: ‘Rickrolled’ in exam hall? CBSE maths paper QR code leads to viral song
content: A QR code on CBSE Class 12 math papers unexpectedly led students to a Rick Astley music video, sparking concerns about exam paper authenticity. While the Board confirmed the papers are genuine and security remains intact, they are investigating the incident to prevent future occurrences. This unusual prank caused significant student and parent anxiety.

title: 'Mujhe bhool gye kya?': Priya Saroj teases Rinku Singh at airport after T20 World Cup glory - watch
content: India's T20 World Cup 2026 victory sees Rinku Singh return home, sharing a lighthearted airport moment with fiancée Priya Saroj.  Despite a personal struggle following his father's passing and a challenging tournament performance, Rinku's dedication to fulfilling his father's dream resonated deeply.  Sanju Samson's impactful contributions proved crucial for India's triumph.

title: ‘She braved cancer for this’: How a retired couple turned their car into a moving home
content: Retirement is redefined by Vinod and Rashmi Alang, who embarked on a 52-day road trip from Delhi to Kanyakumari after their retirements. Despite facing a health crisis, their shared passion for travel and nature's healing power led them to create unique homestays, enhancing their happiness and appreciation for life.

title: 'Don't get eliminated yourself': Iran warns Trump, slaps down 'empty' threats
content: Iran's security official warned President Trump to be careful after the US leader threatened harsher retaliation for any Strait of Hormuz disruption.  Tehran continues regional attacks, with missile and drone strikes hitting Dubai, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait. Iran's parliament speaker indicated no ceasefire is sought, emphasizing retaliation against aggressors.  Global oil prices have surged due to the conflict.

title: India slides down in 2026 GECI rankings: How can it be improved?
content: India slipped to 25th in the 2026 Gender Equality in Chess Index (GECI), highlighting gender imbalance in the sport. Despite strong performances by elite women players, female participation in youth championships remains low, and many girls drop out early. Experts say social pressures, financial hurdles and lack of structured support limit opportunities, stressing the need for better grassroots development and long-term systems to promote women’s chess.

title: Mumtaz links Feroz Khan’s death to Fardeen Khan, Natasha’s separation
content: Veteran actress Mumtaz candidly discussed her daughter Natasha Madhvani and Fardeen Khan's marital issues, suggesting Feroz Khan's passing might have contributed to their drift. Despite living apart, they remain connected co-parents, with Mumtaz praising Fardeen's dedication to their children and his care for Natasha when she was unwell.

title: Vijay Deverakonda shares moments with Rashmika from mehendi & Pradhanam ceremonies
content: Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna shared heartwarming glimpses from their Mehendi and Pradhanam ceremonies, expressing a wish for more time together.  The couple looked radiant in elegant traditional attire, with Rashmika stunning in an embellished earthy-toned outfit and Vijay in royal robes.  Fans were treated to a behind-the-scenes look at their joyous pre-wedding celebrations, filled with laughter and family.

title: One terrorist killed, operation under way along LoC in J&K's Nowshera
content: Indian Army troops thwarted an infiltration attempt along the Line of Control in Jammu and Kashmir's Jhangar sector on Tuesday. Acting on intelligence, alert soldiers engaged two terrorists, successfully eliminating one Pakistan-sponsored infiltrator and preventing any breach of the LoC. A search operation is underway for the second terrorist.

title: 'Dream is to play senior World Cup': Yash Dhull opens up on heart surgery and comeback
content: Under-19 World Cup-winning captain Yash Dhull faced a challenging period after heart surgery, battling a strong desire to return to cricket.  Despite initial setbacks and a risky recovery, Dhull's focus remains on regaining fitness and aiming for a senior World Cup appearance, drawing inspiration from senior players and preparing for future opportunities.

title: Global fuel crisis looms: Gulf nations cut oil output; Hormuz disruptions choke supply
content: The Strait of Hormuz closure is forcing Gulf nations to slash oil production as storage tanks fill. Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, and Iraq have significantly reduced output, with Iraq facing an imminent shutdown. Alternative routes are insufficient, raising fears of a total production halt and impacting global oil prices.

title: Randeep Hooda, Lin Laishram become parents to a baby girl
content: Actor Randeep Hooda and wife Lin Laishram welcomed their first child, a baby girl, on March 10. Hooda shared the joyous news, expressing gratitude to his wife and father. The couple had announced their pregnancy in November last year, sharing their excitement for their 'little wild one'.

title: How much has US-Iran war hit India's oil, LPG and LNG supply? Top 10 points to know
content: India, which is the world’s third-largest importer and consumer of oil, maintains underground strategic petroleum reserves with a capacity of 5.33 million tonnes. However, these reserves are currently filled to about 80 per cent.

title: Ahead of Tamil Nadu polls, Cabinet approves international airport status for Madurai
content: The Union Cabinet has approved declaring Madurai airport an international airport, a move aimed at boosting global connectivity to the temple city. This decision is expected to enhance air connectivity, strengthen Madurai's role as an educational and healthcare hub, and attract international pilgrims and businesses, supporting regional trade and economic activity.

title: Chhattisgarh Tribal woman lynched over witchcraft suspicion; husband, 3 others held
content: A tribal woman in Chhattisgarh's Jashpur district was allegedly killed in a superstition-linked attack after being accused of witchcraft. Her husband, co-wife, and two others have been arrested for the brutal assault, which police believe was motivated by accusations of practising sorcery and causing family misfortunes.

title: Arshdeep Singh penalised by ICC following India's T20 World Cup triumph
content: Indian pacer Arshdeep Singh has been fined 15% of his match fee and received one demerit point for a Level 1 breach of the ICC Code of Conduct. The incident occurred during the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand when he aggressively threw the ball, hitting batter Daryl Mitchell. Arshdeep accepted the sanction, avoiding a formal hearing.

title: Iran war impact on India: LPG shortage concerns rise; which states may feel the heat?
content: West Asia tensions are disrupting commercial LPG supplies to eateries in Bengaluru, Mumbai, and Chennai. Authorities are prioritizing domestic use, impacting businesses. The government is ramping up LPG production, extending refill cycles, and forming a committee to address commercial supply requests amid import concerns.

title: Iran war: Oil surge, midterms worry Trump advisers as they push for an exit plan
content: Amidst surging oil prices and upcoming mid-term elections, President Trump has reportedly been advised to seek an exit plan from the Middle East. Advisors are urging him to articulate that military objectives have largely been achieved, as polls indicate public opposition to the ongoing conflict.

title: Barnwell: Sorting out the winners, losers and dominoes from NFL free agency's wild opening day
content: Tua Tagovailoa in a great spot? No real help for C.J. Stroud? A wildly player-friendly deal for Tyler Linderbaum? Here's the best and worst of free agency.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts and rumors
content: NFL free agency opened Monday with the legal negotiating window. We're following all the moves as they happen.

title: Kerr again calls for league to shorten season
content: Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr said Monday that the NBA would be better off if it shaved games off its 82-game schedule, echoing previous comments he has made about the pace of play and long season affecting its quality and competitiveness.

title: Arozarena unleashes Raleigh rant over WBC snub
content: Randy Arozarena had vulgar words for Mariners teammate Cal Raleigh after he was left hanging at the World Baseball Classic.

title: Reds' Greene to have surgery, targets July return
content: Reds ace Hunter Greene will have an arthroscopic procedure Wednesday to remove bone chips from his right elbow and is not expected to return until July.

title: No. 4 hoops recruit Holt commits to Lloyd, Arizona
content: Caleb Holt, the No. 4 prospect in the 2026 SC Next 100, has committed to Arizona, becoming Tommy Lloyd's highest-ranked recruit since taking over in Tucson.

title: NCAA will use tech to eye tourney refs for betting
content: The NCAA announced Tuesday that it will monitor basketball, baseball and softball tournament officials for betting using a technology called ProhiBet.

title: Koepka wants 'consistency' in his game at Players
content: Books Koepka, who will be making just his fourth start in his return to the PGA Tour, is hoping his game shows "more consistency" this week at the Players Championship.

title: Golfer Woodland opens up about PTSD diagnosis
content: Gary Woodland said Monday he has been battling post-traumatic stress disorder in the wake of his 2023 brain surgery, telling Golf Channel in an emotional interview that he "can't waste energy" trying to hide it anymore.

title: 📋 Field Yates' latest mini NFL mock draft
content: How has NFL free agency affected 2026 draft plans? Here are Field Yates' updated predictions for picks Nos. 1-10 after the first wave of deals.

content: The Broncos could go toe-to-toe with the rest of the bubble, but beating Saint Mary's may give them an edge.

content: Even after Paige Bueckers departed for the WNBA, the Huskies kept rolling and are closing in on a repeat.

title: How the schedule could impact three key NBA playoff races
content: With so many teams tanking, some playoff-bound teams could capitalize on easy schedules down the stretch.

title: From college QB to Team USA starter in the WBC: The incredible rise of Nolan McLean
content: Eight starts into his MLB career, the Mets' budding ace is making himself at home next to Paul Skenes and Tarik Skubal in the U.S. rotation.

title: Why the Dolphins released Tua Tagovailoa, and how he fits with the Falcons
content: If Michael Penix Jr. isn't ready for the season, the Falcons have a new lefty QB to start. Here are the key questions about the move.

title: Women's Bracketology: How did the Power 4 finals affect the bracket?
content: UConn dominated, Texas locked up the No. 3 overall seed, Duke moved to a No. 2 and West Virginia should host NCAA tournament games.

title: Champ Week takeaways: Should UConn or UCLA be No. 1 overall?
content: A week before Selection Sunday, which teams should be worried (Tennessee) or encouraged (Notre Dame)?

title: What to expect from Rosen, Jiricek, other top NHL prospects traded at the deadline
content: How will those players impact their new teams? When will they play meaningful minutes at the NHL level? Here are scouting reports on 10 big names.

title: Predicting the winner of the UEFA Champions League based on past winners
content: Which team is going to win the UEFA Champions League when it's all over in May? If we look at past winners, we might find some important clues.

title: Real Madrid have a gaping, Rodri-sized hole in their midfield
content: Madrid will get a good look at the world's best midfield generals when they face Rodri's Man City in the UCL.

title: NWSL ambition rankings: Which teams are raising standards, and which aren't trying enough?
content: The NWSL has teams that are building stadiums, signing stars and spending money. Meanwhile, other teams, well, aren't doing any of that.

title: 'Man, just please come back safe': What it's like for an MLB team when its stars are in the WBC
content: While its top players are out taking on the world, we checked in on life back at home during spring training.

title: On the rise? Teams, players poised to improve in 2026
content: From potential CFP contenders to bounce-back candidates, we've got good feelings about these programs.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: What if a deal isn't reached by Tuesday's 'deadline'?
content: The WNBA and the union exchanged proposals over the weekend, but there's no sign a deal is imminent. How far apart are both sides?

title: Is 23 red cards in Brazilian mass brawl a world record? Not even close
content: A lot of red cards were handed out in Cruzeiro's win over Atlético-MG, but it's nowhere near the world record.

title: Despite wingback depth, Sergiño Dest injury threatens USMNT's World Cup hopes
content: Sergiño Dest insists his season isn't over after suffering a hamstring injury, but if it is, that's a headache the U.S. doesn't need for the World Cup.

title: Predicting the USWNT's World Cup starting lineup
content: With 2027 closer than you think, we forecast the 11 players Emma Hayes will count on for the tournament.

title: Boxing divisional rankings: Jai Opetaia keeps top spot and claims new title
content: Jai Opetaia beat Brandon Glanton to keep his No. 1 spot at cruiserweight. Here are the latest ESPN divisional rankings.

title: 2026 Players Championship: Ranking the top 25 players in the field
content: We run through the top contenders among the 123-man field to win the PGA Tour's flagship event at TPC Sawgrass.

title: Eric Karabell's 'Do not draft' list
content: Eric Karabell identifies the players unlikely to be on his roster this season due to too much risk, too much hype or not enough at-bats.

title: News or noise: Reds ace Hunter Greene shelved for months
content: Eric Karabell keeps tabs on the baseball world and tells you what is relevant to fantasy baseball managers.

title: Australian GP overreactions: Verstappen could quit F1? McLaren on the ropes?
content: After a wild, often polarising Australian Grand Prix weekend, ESPN looks at some of the sweeping generalisations being made about Formula 1 and judges whether they're overblown or not.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal, Barcelona linked with out-of-contract Dortmund star
content: Arsenal and Barcelona are keen on Borussia Dortmund's Julian Brandt, while Real Madrid have again been linked with Manchester City's Rodri. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Opposition walks out of Rajya Sabha as debate on Bengal voter roll revision is denied
content: As Opposition parties raise the Special Intensive Revision in West Bengal, Leader of the House and Union Minister J.P. Nadda reminds them there was a detailed debate on electoral reforms

title: Mass suspensions, mics switched off: Opposition talking points in debate on resolution to remove Speaker
content: Opposition MPs also pull up the government over the failure to appoint a Deputy Speaker for last 12 years

title: Mamata ends SIR stir, says game is not yet over
content: During her five-day protest, the West Bengal Chief Minister and her party aides targeted the Election Commission of India; the Commission is working as ‘an agent of the BJP to snatch voting rights away’ from the people of the State, she says

title: Watch: U.S-Israeli-Iran war: Why oil prices are suddenly surging worldwide| Explained
content: The war between the United States, Israel and Iran is beginning to shake global energy markets. After air strikes on Iran, Tehran responded with missile attacks and announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz — one of the world’s most critical oil shipping routes.

title: Cabinet nod for 23% hike in cost of link road from Noida airport to Delhi-Mumbai Expressway
content: Revised cost of project is ₹3,630 cr; project to be developed on Hybrid Annuity Mode in Haryana, U.P.; 11 km of 31-km link road will be an elevated highway

title: Don’t carry out any work on ground for now on Sharavathi pumped storage hydroelectric project, Karnataka High Court directs State government
content: A Division Bench comprising Chief Justice Vibhu Bakhru and Justice C.M. Poonacha passed the interim order on the petition filed by Akhilesh Chipli of Shivamogga and two others.

title: Dermatologists warn against rise in unqualified cosmetic procedures
content: Indian Association of Dermatologists, Venereologists and Leprologists Karnataka branch released a QR code that allows members of the public to identify qualified dermatologists in Karnataka.

title: SFC recommends 5% of GST revenue allocation to municipal bodies, including GBA corporations
content: The Fifth State Finance Commission says such a measure will help increase the revenue of municipal bodies by 52%

title: BRS to introduce Private Member Bill in Assembly seeking legal backing to six guarantees
content: Working president of BRS KTR says half of the Congress government’s tenure lapsed with most of the promises remaining unkept

title: Six persons honoured with IIT-M’s First Principles Teachers Award 2026
content: The awards were presented to school teachers for their contributions to teaching of physics, chemistry, and math, thereby building strong foundations for future engineers

title: Nurses’ strike continues to affect private hospitals in Kerala
content: Functioning of key departments in some of the major institutions in districts such as Kozhikode has been severely affected and the patients are left in the lurch

title: Supreme Court asks for special tribunals to hear appeals of voters excluded in West Bengal SIR
content: The Supreme Court asked the Chief Justice of Calcutta High Court to request former Chief Justices and judges of High Courts to preside over the Benches of the appellate tribunals once they are notified by the Election Commission

title: Cabinet approves two rail projects
content: These projects include a fourth rail line between Santragachi – Kharagpur in West Bengal and another connecting Sainthia in West Bengal and Pakur in Jharkhand

title: Nitish outlines five-year vision for Bihar during last leg of Samriddhi Yatra
content: During his public meetings in Supaul and Madhepura district, Kumar did not utter a word about going to Rajya Sabha or leaving the post of Chief Minister

title: Air India Fares To Rise As It Imposes Fuel Surcharge Amid Iran War
content: Air India will introduce a phased increase in fuel surcharges across its domestic and international network, the airline announced today, citing a sharp rise in jet fuel prices driven by the Iran-US war in the Middle East.

title: Opinion: Opinion |  This Poll Season, Mamata Banerjee's Hindu Outreach Goes The Extra Mile
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has been on an overdrive for about a year, to announce and inaugurate temples in various corners of the state, amid BJP allegations that she is "appeasing" the minority  community.

title: India Defines 4 Priority Sectors For Using Natural Gas Amid Iran-US War
content: The Iran-US war in the Middle East has affected the supply of natural gas across the world. The Strait of Hormuz remains a part of the war zone for over two weeks, due to which more than 750 cargo ships are stuck at major ports in the region.

title: Dubai's Spice, Dry Fruit Trade Feels Heat Of Iran War: NDTV Ground Report
content: Dubai's historic spice and dry fruit markets, a major global hub for trade of saffron, nuts, dates and spices, are beginning to feel the impact of war in Iran as Tehran bombs US targets in the Middle East.

title: Video: Iran Deploys Lego-Style Animation In Propaganda War With US, Israel
content: While Iran hits back against US and Israeli attacks with missiles and drones, it is also fighting a propaganda war with a Lego-style animation video complete with toy renditions...

title: Eye On China, Centre Eases Foreign Direct Investment Rules For Neighbours
content: The rules for foreign investment by neighbouring countries - put in place amid the Covid pandemic to protect domestic firms - has finally been relaxed by the government.

title: Iran War Delivers Fresh Shockwave To Global Economy
content: The conflict is driving up energy and fertiliser prices, threatening food shortages in poor countries, destabilising fragile states such as Pakistan, and complicating options for the inflation fighters at central banks like the Federal Reserve.

title: "Terrifying Fantasy Games": Martin Wolf To NDTV On Iran War
content: "The whole thing to me now feels like fantasy games, terrifying. I hope I am wrong but I don't see how this ends because I don't see any rationality," Marti Wolf said

title: CEO Pieter Elbers Quits With "Immediate Effect" 3 Months After IndiGo Crisis
content: IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers has resigned and Managing Director Rahul Bhatia has been appointed interim charge of management of the affairs of the company, the airline said in regulatory filing today.

title: "I Don't Believe He Can Live In Peace": Trump's Warning For Mojtaba Khamenei
content: US President Donald Trump said that Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei will not be able to "live in peace". Trump has expressed strong disapproval after Iran elected the second son of Ali Khamenei as the new leader.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei: Iran's New Supreme Leader, Often Seen As A 'Gatekeeper'
content: Mojtaba Khamenei, the 56-year-old son of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is a low-profile cleric who has spent most of his career outside public office but close to power, working within the Office of the Supreme Leader.

title: Iran's Big Gamble With Khamenei's Son Majotaba's Elevation As Supreme Leader
content: Iran's clerical leadership chose confrontation over compromise in appointing Mojtaba Khamenei to succeed his father, Ali Khamenei, a move regional officials say is a direct rebuke to US President Donald Trump, who had declared the son "unacceptable".

title: Gold, Silver Prices Edge Higher: Check Today's Rates In Your City
content: Gold, Silver Prices Today: Gold and silver prices edged higher on Tuesday, tracking gains in international bullion markets.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The One Big Reason This Indian T20i Team Is A Nightmare For The Rest
content: Overall, India have way more match-winners in their line-up than any other team. In fact, such is the depth of India's T20i resources that they can field two or three separate teams, and they will all be world-class.

title: Temporary LPG Crisis After Hormuz blockade Resolved: Sources
content: The production of cooking gas LPG is up by 10 per cent across oil refineries after the government issued directions yesterday, government sources have told NDTV.

title: Form Compensation Policy For Harm From Covid Shots: Supreme Court To Centre
content: The Supreme Court on Tuesday directed the Centre to formulate a no-fault compensation policy for people who suffered serious adverse events following Covid-19 vaccination.

title: Terrorist Killed As Army Foils Infiltration Attempt In J&K's Rajouri
content: Army troops foiled an infiltration bid along the Line of Control (LoC) in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri district on Tuesday, killing one terrorist during an encounter, officials said.

title: "Main Hoon Na": IndiGo's Interim Boss To Employees After CEO's Resignation
content: IndiGo's Managing Director Rahul Bhatia, who took interim charge of the airline after CEO Pieter Elbers quit, has written to employees a pep talk of sorts on the developments.

title: "Fridge Mein Milegi": Mumbai Dentist's Suicide, And Shraddha Walkar Warning
content: A 24-year-old student of dentistry has died by suicide at her Mumbai home, allegedly due to mental harassment by her boyfriend.

title: Pieter Elbers: The Top Executive Who Headed IndiGo During Its Worst Crisis
content: Pieter Elbers resigned today as Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of IndiGo, India's largest airline, with immediate effect, citing personal reasons.

title: Bangladesh receives diesel from India via pipeline
content: Earlier, Bangladesh said India would send 5,000 tonnes of diesel under a long-term agreement and as part of an annual arrangement to deliver 180,000 tonnes

title: Biodiversity Study Centre at Kidoor inaugurated
content: The facility, developed as part of the Kumbla rural tourism project under the Kasaragod Development Package, was inaugurated by Manjeswaram MLA A.K.M. Ashraf

title: Ratna Bharat Raitha Samaj to stage protest over problems caused by Benne Halla
content: The samaj will hold the protest in Dharwad on Thursday demanding that the State govt. release ₹3,000 crore to provide a permanent solution

title: India’s LNG & LPG shortage to be short-lived as new supply routes, domestic production kick in: Govt sources
content: Government sources say importing natural gas from U.S. & Norway has become economically viable despite the distance, while domestic LPG production has been ramped up

title: Three govt. employees terminated for ‘anti-national’ activities in J&K
content: Shaleen Kabra, financial commissioner, Jal Shakti Department, directed ‘disengagement’ from service of three employees

title: Gulf tensions trigger LPG supply concerns in Andhra Pradesh
content: Domestic stocks adequate for 25 to 30 days, say officials as hotels report restrictions on commercial cylinder refills amid shipping uncertainty near Strait of Hormuz

title: Surendran’s possible candidature reportedly sparks differences in BJP’s Manjeswaram unit
content: K. Surendran reportedly steps up activities in the constituency amid indications he may contest from Manjeswaram

title: Early detection of forest fires critical for protecting biodiversity-rich habitats: study
content: WWF India’s temporal analysis shows that fires in Idukki are strongly seasonal, with the majority of incidents occurring during the January–April dry season

title: Building collapse: Kozhikode Corporation sets aside internal report, seeks NIT-C safety audit
content: Mayor says that once the NIT-C report is received, buildings earmarked for demolition will be razed, while those requiring reinforcement will be strengthened and maintained

title: Bharath urges HPV vaccination for girls, utilisation of govt. health services
content: Minister participates in the free vaccination camp for girls aged between 14 and 15, which kicked off in Kurnool

title: Lok Sabha takes up resolution seeking removal of Speaker Om Birla
content: Parliamentary Minister Kiren Rijiju says resolution is an attack on democratic tradtions; Congress MP Gaurav Gogoi says the move is meant to protect the neutrality of the institution of the Speaker

title: No shortage of LPG in State, says Civil Supplies Minister

title: Duke's Foster out indefinitely with foot fracture

title: Dolphins signing Malik Willis to replace Tua: Answering 6 key questions
content: the Miami Dolphins agree to a three-year, $67.5 million deal with quarterback malik Willis . the deal presumably makes him the team's starting quarterback for at least the 2026 season . first-year general manager Jon-Eric Sullivan played coy with his interest in him at the NFL combine .
